text.title: AI Accessibility Automation 2026 Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the ai-accessibility-automation-2026 methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Pipeline MUST combine deterministic scanners (axe + Lighthouse) AND AI triage.
rule.rationale: Scanner alone surfaces high false-positive volume.

rule.statement: Pipeline output MUST mark issues 'requires AT testing' for any criterion scanners cannot evaluate (cognitive, voice navigation, screen-reader semantics).
rule.rationale: Without the gate teams ship 'green CI' that fails real AT users.

rule.statement: Severity classification MUST come from AI triage, not scanner default.
Critical-severity issues MUST fail CI; non-critical issues MUST appear in the report.
Every CI run MUST persist scan + triage + verdict for ≥90 days.
When preconditions hold, run the full procedure end-to-end and emit the output artefact.
rule.rationale: Routes positive decision-tree leaves to the procedure.

rule.statement: When preconditions fail or skip-conditions hold, exit without producing the artefact and document why.
rule.rationale: Routes negative decision-tree leaves out without polluting the output channel.
